my_thoughts: The mechanical objection works: fear of losing money/identification (cannot work without identification, will lose all money), reiterates: no connection between interest and discomfort. The student asserted positive consequence (clinging to utilitarian hope), which shows they understand: discomfort = get rid of identification (positive consequence). They'll likely report observation next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student asserted positive consequence ('They will get rid of identification')—clinging to utilitarian hope via asserts positive consequence and defends premise. They understand: discomfort = get rid of identification (positive consequence). The system_dynamics mechanism gives mechanical objection: fear of losing money/identification (cannot work without identification, will lose all money), reiterates: no connection between interest and discomfort ('And they will say that they cannot work without identification, and if they are not identified, they will lose all their money. There is no connection between interest in the system and the discomfort of the present situation'). Pattern: causal link proposal → rejection → utilitarian assertion → mechanical objection → [next: observation question]. They're grasping: mechanical resistance = fear of losing money/identification (mechanical objection).
